name: BMP to PNG
summary: Zero-dependency conversion of supported Windows BMP rasters to streamed RGB/RGBA PNG.

scope: Windows DIB headers from BITMAPINFOHEADER through bounded extended headers
BI_RGB, BI_BITFIELDS, BI_ALPHABITFIELDS, BI_RLE4 and BI_RLE8
Top-down and bottom-up uncompressed row order
out_of_scope: ICC profile and general BMP metadata migration

guarantees: Accepted BMP pixels are emitted in the same top-down spatial order
Declared RGB and alpha channel values are mapped deterministically to eight-bit PNG channels
PNG chunks, CRC-32, zlib framing and Adler-32 are emitted natively
tradeoffs: Palette representation, BMP padding and row orientation are normalized
BMP metadata is not migrated in version 0.1

properties: Test PNG output is independently inflated and unfiltered before pixel comparison
Both native Deflate strategies produce one valid zlib stream across arbitrary IDAT chunk boundaries
regression: Bottom-up rows become top-down PNG scanlines
Unmarked 32-bit alpha is opaque by default
RGB565 bitfields scale channel endpoints exactly
fuzz: Planned: header, palette, bitfield and RLE command fuzz campaigns
benchmarks: Planned: entropy and row-filter corpus across both compression strategies

limits: DIB headers are bounded to 4096 bytes
IDAT chunks are bounded to 16 MiB
All dimensions, offsets and allocations use checked arithmetic
known_risks: Seek or output I/O errors may occur after partial PNG output
RLE input allocates one decoded palette index per pixel
